name: Midland Municipal Employees
description_short: Midland States Bank offers checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and wealth management services for personal, small business, and commercial customers through online and branch banking.
description_long:
Midland States Bank is a full-service financial institution providing comprehensive banking and financial services to personal, small business, and commercial customers. The company operates through both digital and physical branch locations, offering customers multiple channels to access their services and support.

The bank's product suite includes personal checking and savings accounts (Totally Free Checking, Direct Interest Checking, High Yield Checking), personal and business loans, mortgages, credit cards, and wealth management services including trust, investments, and advisory services through Raymond James and Epic Advisor partnerships. They also provide HSA accounts and a referral program offering up to $500 for successful customer referrals.

Midland distinguishes itself through emphasis on customer service relationships, highlighting staff like 'Lynette' in testimonials, combined with digital accessibility through online banking, mobile banking, and online tools. They offer financial calculators, tutorials, a financial solution finder tool, and multiple contact methods (phone, email, chat). The bank actively markets account opening bonuses of up to $100 for new checking and savings accounts with qualifying criteria.

As a traditional community bank, Midland provides standard banking services comparable to regional and national banks. While their website demonstrates professional service offerings and digital tools, the company primarily serves customers seeking conventional banking relationships rather than specialized financial solutions. Their strength lies in offering comprehensive services under one roof for customers wanting traditional banking with personal service. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Totally Free Checking accounts with no minimum balance
Direct Interest Checking with interest earnings
Small business loans and lines of credit
Credit cards for personal and business use
Investment advisory through Raymond James partnership
pros: No-fee checking option with no minimum balance requirement (Totally Free Checking)
Multiple checking account tiers with interest-earning options (Direct Interest Checking and High Yield Checking)
Comprehensive wealth management services including trust, investments, and advisory partnerships
Account opening bonuses up to $100 for new checking and savings accounts with direct deposit
Referral program offering up to $500 in earnings for referring new customers
24/7 digital access through online and mobile banking with step-by-step tutorials
Multiple contact channels including phone (1-855-696-4352), email, chat, and in-person appointments
cons: Website does not clearly disclose specific APR ranges or rates for checking, savings, loans, or mortgages
No prominent information about minimum deposits, account maintenance requirements, or overdraft fee structures
Limited transparency about wealth management fee structures and advisor compensation models
best_for: Small business owners seeking integrated banking, lending, and payroll services
Individuals wanting comprehensive banking (checking, savings, loans, mortgages) from a single provider
Customers prioritizing personal relationships with dedicated banking professionals over digital-only options
diagnosis: Midland States Bank is best for customers seeking traditional full-service banking with personal relationships, comprehensive products, and digital convenience. The main caveat is that the website lacks transparent pricing details on rates, fees, and account requirements, requiring prospective customers to contact the bank directly for specific terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
